_note: Registry of companies whose financials + ratios have been hand-verified correct (read from the filing, accounting identities checked, and cross-checked against PSX and/or Sarmaaya). 'throughPeriod' is the latest fiscal period verified — once a newer filing lands, the mark is stale and the name needs re-verification. 'basis' is the reporting basis we store (unconsolidated matches PSX; holding-cos also noted). Add entries only after an actual check, not on assumption.

OGDC.note: EPS 36.17 = Sarmaaya exact; P/E 9.64 (TTM) vs PSX 8.93 annual

MTL.note: 100% bonus; share-count override 399.03M; P/E 16.44 vs Sarmaaya 16.41

LUCK.note: holding-co; both bases stored; PSX vendor P/E was wrong, ours is right P/B was found to have been silently basis-mixed (an accidental tie-break picked the consolidated balance sheet against unconsolidated EPS, giving 1.48) -- now correctly basis-consistent at 3.19, which diverges further from Sarmaaya's 1.77 because Sarmaaya's P/B reference is on the consolidated basis, same reason our unconsolidated P/E doesn't match PSX vendor P/E either. Not investigated further; the EPS/P-E claim this entry certifies is unaffected.

GWLC.note: cement; hand-read added the missing 9M FY2025 comparative (EPS 3.14). TTM 6.75 vs Sarmaaya 6.74, P/E 8.15 vs 8.17 — near-exact. Retained-earnings roll ties to the filing

POWER.note: cement; 9M FY2025 was absent and the FY2025 quarterly rows had been copied from FY2026. Real 9M FY2025 EPS 0.07, not the 1.47 those duplicates implied. TTM 2.28 vs Sarmaaya 2.24

FECTC.note: cement; no 9M FY2026 row existed (H1 plus a stray Q3) and 9M FY2025 was missing. TTM 14.77 vs Sarmaaya 14.00 — residual 5.5% is the FY2025 annual restatement, which we still hold at the original 12.14

DCL.note: cement; loss-making. Quarantined a sign-flipped duplicate 2025 Q1 (+0.82 vs the real -0.82) and a bogus H1. TTM -2.37 = Sarmaaya EXACT

DNCC.note: cement; loss-making micro-cap. Added the missing 9M FY2025 comparative (EPS -0.31). TTM -0.39 = Sarmaaya EXACT. P/E not meaningful on a loss

THCCL.note: cement; 5:1 split (Rs 10 -> Rs 2 par) — FY2025 EPS restated 30.18 -> 6.04, Q3 6.61 -> 1.32, and the missing 9M FY2025 comparative added at 3.98. TTM 6.54. Sarmaaya's 7.24 is consolidated (group 9M EPS 5.17 vs our 4.48)

SMCPL.note: cement; filed in full rupees, converted to thousands. Added the missing 9M FY2025 comparative (EPS 2.12) and quarantined a duplicated Q1. TTM 6.01. No Sarmaaya ratios published, but EPS 4.02 ties exactly to PAT 100,479,745 / 25M shares

FATIMA.note: fertilizer; Dec year-end. Filing states both bases: standalone Q1-2026 EPS 1.99 on PAT 4,189m and Q1-2025 restated 3.84 (Multan Plant carveout to Pakarab, effective 1 Jan 2025) — both match our rows exactly. TTM 12.66 is correct unconsolidated; Sarmaaya's 17.58 is the group (consolidated Q1-2026 EPS 1.54). P/B 2.07 vs Sarmaaya 2.01

EFERT.note: fertilizer; Dec year-end. Unconsolidated Q1-2026 net sales 26,338,683 / PAT 2,891,066 / EPS 2.17 and Q1-2025 EPS 2.94 confirmed against the filing; TTM 17.03. Hand-read the missing 2026 Q1 balance sheet (equity 42,509,710 + liabilities 157,049,968 = assets 199,559,678), which had been silently falling back to the Dec 2025 sheet — P/B corrected 5.71 to 6.04

SBL.note: bank; agent-read filing corrected FY2025 unconsolidated EPS (was stored 'unlabelled' at a different value) and added the missing 2025 Q1 comparative. Rebuilt TTM 0.74 vs Sarmaaya 0.75, page-cited (FY p36, Q1s p13). 'agent+Sarmaaya' source distinguishes this from a human-read filing (filing+Sarmaaya) — the model read it and the figures were independently rebuilt and checked here, but no person opened the PDF.

BAHL.note: bank; Sarmaaya quotes the group figure. Agent-read filing gave FY2025 29.19 (p177), Q1 2025 9.65 (p58), Q1 2026 6.54 (p58), all consolidated, rebuilding to TTM 26.08 exact to Sarmaaya. Ratio engine gained a per-company basis preference (CONSOLIDATED_BASIS_TICKERS in lib/engine/ratios.ts) to serve this — confirmed via getRatioCard (the actual API path), P/E now eps=26.08 exactly, P/B 1.22 vs Sarmaaya 1.21 as an independent second check. Zero regressions across the other 49 verified companies on re-check. Confirmed via getRatioCard (the actual API path), P/E now eps=26.08 exactly. P/B is currently unavailable (not wrong): the balance-sheet selection was later made basis-aware too, and no consolidated balance sheet has been captured for BAHL yet, so the engine correctly declines to pair the consolidated EPS with an unconsolidated book value rather than silently mixing bases. Zero regressions across the other verified companies on re-check.

FCCL.note: cement; agent read the annual report's quarterly-breakdown table (page 210, FY2025: Q1 1.32, Q2 1.64, Q3 0.87, Q4 1.59). Found our stored FY2025 H1 and Q2 were swapped (H1 held 1.64 which is actually Q2 alone; Q2 held 2.96 which is actually Q1+Q2) and Q1 2025 was missing entirely. Corrected via the quarter-sum fallback (no direct 9M row needed): 9M FY2025 = 1.32+1.64+0.87 = 3.83. Rebuilt TTM = 5.43 + 4.39 - 3.83 = 5.99, exact to Sarmaaya's 5.99. P/E and P/B both track ~8% low vs the Sarmaaya snapshot, fully explained by price movement between the snapshot date and our live quote (57.65 -> 53.74), not an EPS issue.

MUGHAL.note: steel; Sarmaaya quotes the group figure. My inventory builder had grabbed the wrong interim document (a Shariah-disclosure resubmission with no P&L, title-matched on 'half yearly accounts') -- fixed the filter, re-fetched the real quarterly report, and read both filings by hand (agent's earlier passes never produced a complete chain). Consolidated FY2025 EPS 2.50 (annual report p153), 9M 2026 5.10 and 9M 2025 comparative 1.23 (interim Directors' Review p3). Rebuilt TTM = 2.50+5.10-1.23 = 6.37, exact to Sarmaaya. Unconsolidated TTM (7.24, all figures independently confirmed on the interim's own P&L p6) does not reconcile -- this is a genuine basis difference, not a data error; our unconsolidated figures were correct all along. P/B is currently unavailable (not wrong): no consolidated balance sheet has been captured for MUGHAL, so the engine correctly declines to pair the consolidated EPS with an unconsolidated book value. (An earlier version of this note cited P/B 0.89 vs Sarmaaya 0.91 -- that number came from an unconsolidated balance sheet paired with a consolidated EPS, a basis mix the engine no longer allows once balance-sheet selection was made basis-aware too.)

SEARL.note: pharma; Sarmaaya quotes the group figure. FY2025 was a genuine consolidated LOSS per share of -2.73 (annual report p217), driven by a Rs 2.18bn discontinued-operations loss on divesting subsidiary Searle Pakistan Ltd -- not a data error, an actual one-off event. That figure predates a 15% bonus issued during 9M FY2026 and was never restated; the interim's own comparatives ARE restated per IAS 33, so combined them at face value would double-count the dilution on one side only. Restated FY2025 to the post-bonus share base (511,494,424 -> 588,218,587, both from the filings' own share-capital notes): -2.73 * (511494424/588218587) = -2.37. TTM = -2.37 + 3.89(9M 2026, p24) - (-0.55)(9M 2025 comparative, p28) = 2.07, within 1.5% of Sarmaaya's 2.04. P/B is currently unavailable (not wrong): no consolidated balance sheet has been captured for SEARL, so the engine correctly declines to pair the consolidated EPS with an unconsolidated book value.

GAL.note: auto assembler; both filings exceeded the vision provider's combined payload limit (26.7MB annual + 1.8MB interim, HTTP 413), so read by hand instead of forcing a degraded chunked read. Sarmaaya quotes the group (99.9%-owned subsidiary Ghandhara DF). Consolidated FY2025 EPS 71.85 (annual report p133), 9M 2026 EPS 85.28 and 9M 2025 comparative 39.89 (interim p5/p23-24). TTM = 71.85+85.28-39.89 = 117.24, exact to Sarmaaya. No share-count restatement needed (unchanged 57,002,500 shares, no bonus/rights issue). P/E confirmed via getRatioCard: eps=117.24 exactly. Also found and fixed a real bug this surfaced: GAL's consolidated balance sheet (equity 19,165,213) was stored with reporting_basis 'unlabelled' and review_status 'needs_review', so the ratio engine's un-basis-aware balance-sheet selection silently picked the unconsolidated equity instead, giving P/B 2.55 against Sarmaaya's 1.7. Relabeled+published the row and extended the engine's balance/cash-flow selection to respect CONSOLIDATED_BASIS_TICKERS (previously only the income-statement side was basis-aware) -- P/B now 1.70, exact.

ABOT.note: bulk-promoted on independent agreement, NOT a filing read. Our engine-computed trailing EPS 84.85 vs Sarmaaya 84.85 (0.0%), and P/B 2.72 vs 2.76 (1.5%) — two ratios off different statements agreeing independently. No one opened the filing, so an un-restated bonus/rights issue or a basis subtlety could still be hiding underneath (the failure modes that caught SEARL, MUGHAL and GAL). Weaker tier than the "filing+Sarmaaya"/"hand+Sarmaaya" entries; re-verify by hand before relying on it for anything load-bearing.

Added to CONSOLIDATED_BASIS_TICKERS.

JSBL.note: bank; Sarmaaya quotes the group. Read off the cached PDFs by a subagent using the Read tool, which was the ONLY route available: the filings are scanned/image-only so the DeepSeek text-layer extractor could not touch them, and both vision API providers (OpenRouter, Anthropic) were out of credits. Consolidated FY2025 2.84 (annual printed p183) + Q1'26 0.44 - Q1'25 1.18 (both interim printed p51) = TTM 2.10, exact to Sarmaaya's 2.1. Unconsolidated (1.36 + 0.51 - 0.63 = 1.24) does not reconcile. P/B 0.41 vs Sarmaaya 0.42 as an independent second check, using equity ATTRIBUTABLE TO OWNERS (66,531,130k, interim p50) not total equity including NCI (78,803,779k) -- consolidated EPS is struck on owners' profit, so total equity would mismatch numerator and denominator. Same reason profit_after_tax is stored as 5,826,980k (owners) not 7,539,228k (total incl. 1,712,248k NCI). The automated pass this morning had the two FY figures but not the Q1 comparatives, so it could not build a chain and reported 'neither basis matches' -- the gap was missing data, not bad reasoning.

_unverifiable.DBCI: Dadabhoy Cement Industries (Cement): no filings on record, no balance sheet, no revenue in any period, and a stale quote. A defunct listing that still sits in the live universe — it cannot be verified, only excluded.
_unverifiable.AGL: Agritech (Fertilizer): Q1-2026 EPS -1.70 and Q1-2025 -0.56 are confirmed correct against the filing, but the stored FY2025 annual pair is internally inconsistent — EPS 5.36 x the confirmed 599.08M shares (paid-up capital 5,990,842,240 at Rs 10) implies 3,211m against a stored PAT of 2,895,547k, and AGL has preference dividends that should reduce the ordinary claim further, not raise it. TTM 4.22 is therefore unreliable at roughly the 14% level. Needs the FY2025 annual report read; no Sarmaaya reference exists to arbitrate. Distressed structure regardless: accumulated losses -23.5bn, equity positive only via a 35.4bn revaluation surplus.
_unverifiable.AHCL: Arif Habib Corporation: mis-sectored as Fertilizer — it is a holding company (Power Cement, Aisha Steel, PIA stakes). No annual row exists at all, so P/E 3.68 is being computed off a 9-month EPS rather than a trailing twelve months. Added the real 9M FY2025 comparative (EPS 4.33) and quarantined a duplicated H1. Even once the annual lands, P/E is close to meaningless here: 12.75bn of the 17.12bn 9M profit is unrealised mark-to-market gain on listed investments. Same basis-architecture class as ENGROH.

_needsRecheck._note: Was verified, or was changed by an automated pass, but the trailing figure no longer stands. Re-read the filing before restoring or granting the mark.
_needsRecheck.PSEL: Backfill produced trailing 21.06 against Sarmaaya 5.58 (+277%). Also holds contradictory FY rows (2022 FY at -10.99 and 18.73; 2023 FY at -6.71 and -52.35).
_needsRecheck.AVN: Backfill produced trailing 0.14 against Sarmaaya 1.02 (-86%). Filing 277710 yielded three 2025 FY rows: consolidated 1.51, unconsolidated 0.14, unlabelled 1.54. The 0.14 the engine selected is almost certainly a misread.
_needsRecheck.SIEM: REJECTED a tempting exact match. A consolidated/unconsolidated sweep found FY2025 12.77 + H1'26 1.53 - H1'25 -17.07 = 31.37, exactly Sarmaaya's 31.37 — but that chain is a TTM through H1 2026 while we serve TTM through 9M 2026, so it agrees with a STALE reference rather than proving us right. Worse, the latest real interim we hold is H1 2026, yet the engine serves a 9M built from a 2026 Q3 row that is byte-identical to Q2 — the same duplicate-label shape as FCCL's swapped H1/Q2. Needs the Q3 2026 filing read to establish the true 9M before anything here can be trusted.
_needsRecheck.SFL: consolidated chain FY2025 1640.1 + H1'26 67.89 - H1'25 68.31 = 1639.68 vs Sarmaaya 1680 (2.4%), but it matches on H1 while we serve 9M, and no consolidated 9M exists. Same stale-period ambiguity as SIEM: cannot tell whether the reference is a quarter behind or our 9M is wrong. Needs the filing.

_pendingConsolidatedBasis._note: The ratio engine computes unconsolidated only (its TTM builder explicitly excludes consolidated rows, the guard that fixed the PPL mixed-basis bug). These are companies where the CONSOLIDATED figure is what the reference (Sarmaaya) actually quotes and matches, the consolidated data has been captured and page-cited, but the platform's served P/E still reflects unconsolidated. Not verified, because that would assert something the API does not deliver. Promotable once the engine gains a per-company basis preference. Companies like LUCK/FFC/HUBC are NOT here: for those, unconsolidated IS the correct served figure (matches PSX), Sarmaaya's consolidated number is simply a different convention, and they are already verified under 'verified'.
